base_housing_and_axle.note: housing + motor mount shaft + gear axle pin; mounts to the robot body; FOURTH PASS: per user 2026-07-03, a second motor beside the worm input YAWS this whole assembly left-right on the body mount — small pitch angles are compensated by steering the swing plane

leg_swing_link.note: sector gear + drive frame + guide rails + knee carrier + shin rail + distal carrier: ONE rigid link swinging on the gear axle pin, worm-driven

blade_upper.note: straight center strip, knee pin -> toe hinge; the knee-motor crank (r = 75 mm)

blade_lower.note: curved perforated plates, toe hinge -> heel pin; the conrod (L = 100 mm)

pushrod.note: pure piston (incl. heel pin): the distal-carrier bushing restricts it to up/down motion colinear with the shin rail (no tilt)

yaw_mount_plate.note: CONFIRMED (user 2026-07-03): the yaw pivot is AT the leg-to-body attachment — the entire leg assembly (housing, worm motor, sector gear, everything distal) yaws as one piece on this interface. This 'plate' is the body-side frame; it may own zero meshes in this leg-only export (pure attachment frame). Remaining TODO: yaw ROM + gearing.

leg_swing.note: worm drives the sector gear 20:1; gear grips the drive frame through its C-opening, so frame+rails+leg swing together

knee_blade_flexion.note: KNEE-motor-driven crank; origin in leg-link coords

toe_hinge.note: yellow hinge at the top of the blade joining the two lengths

heel_pin_joint.note: the lower length's ear wraps the heel pin on top of the piston; slider-crank closure - as the blade sweeps out the heel pin lowers toward the ground (weight keeps the joints seated)

pushrod_bushing_slide.note: bushing restricts the rod to piston motion only, colinear with the shin rail and distal carrier

toe_pin_reference.note: blade tip spacer pin, radius 75 mm

hip_yaw.note: CONFIRMED first joint in the chain, pivoting at the body attachment: yaw -> leg_swing (worm 20:1 pitch) -> knee_blade -> passive foot loop. ROM still placeholder ±45°; origin approximated at the attachment/motor-pair location.

hip_worm_drive_motor.status: worm on motor mount shaft; 20:1 from ~20T sector gear

knee_motor.status: CORRECTED (user 2026-07-03): the knee motor ACTIVELY drives the blade down as well as retracting it — deployment is gravity-ASSISTED, not gravity-only (third-pass Drake finding 'restraint and retraction only' understated it). The blade is a POWERED downward strike: slider-crank is a toggle press — near the stowed pose dh/dphi -> 0 so piston force per motor torque -> very large (clamping/pinning); at full extension dh/dphi = 74.6 mm/rad so 3 N.m -> ~40 N at the tip plus the mechanism's weight. Peak strike force arrives at the START of the stomp, easing as it extends.

hip_yaw_motor.transmission: belt + LARGE RUBBER PULLEY on the leg side (user 2026-07-03)
hip_yaw_motor.gear_ratio: TODO: pulley diameter ratio (motor pulley dia / leg pulley dia)
hip_yaw_motor.series_stiffness_nm_per_rad: TODO: torsional stiffness of belt+rubber — bench-measurable (lock motor, twist leg, measure torque/deflection)
hip_yaw_motor.status: SERIES-ELASTIC by design: the rubber pulley is the impact absorber for lateral blows — the deliberate compliant path that the self-locking worm pitch axis lacks. Backdrivable, shock-filtering; costs yaw control bandwidth (steering is springy) — must be modeled as motor->spring/damper->joint, NOT a rigid gear, or sim-trained policies will assume crisper steering than the hardware has.

notes: 48 unnamed flat mesh nodes; groups assigned from measured bounds/colors/shape.
Sector gear rim circle fit: center (-0.16269, 0.00000), R 0.0495, ~20 teeth over ~270 deg.
Design intent per user: drive frame [4] + guide rails [7] are NOT fixed - they swing with the sector gear about the gear axle pin (worm-driven).
A separate KNEE motor at the knee carrier drives the foot blade + pushrod.

The blade is TWO lengths hinged at the yellow toe pin: upper straight strip (knee crank, r 75 mm) and lower curved perforated plates (conrod, L 100 mm) whose ear wraps the heel pin on top of the piston. Slider-crank: as the blade sweeps out the heel pin lowers 41 mm toward the ground, weight keeping the joints seated. The distal-carrier bushing restricts the piston to up/down motion colinear with the shin rail (no tilt).

Knee-blade ROM per design intent: toe pin pivots OUT IN FRONT of the knee pin (-90 deg, blade horizontal toe-forward) with a small +10 deg back-reach; the heel ear swings back through the knee carrier's internal slot region. Swing ROM +/-25 deg. Both verified against rendered frames.
Mass/friction values are placeholders for downstream schema testing.
The GIF is keyframed kinematics, not a Bullet rigid-body simulation.

FOURTH PASS (2026-07-03, from user): a THIRD motor beside the worm input yaws the whole drive assembly left-right — 3 motors/leg (yaw, worm-pitch, knee). Foot workspace becomes a cone: stride can come from yaw sweep while ±25° pitch handles clearance. Mesh membership of the true fixed mount vs the yaw rotor, yaw ROM, and yaw gearing are PENDING confirmation; rig_foot_7_1.py and scripts/drake_leg_analysis.py still model the third pass (no yaw) and need the same joint added once confirmed.

YAW DRIVE (user 2026-07-03): belt system with a large rubber pulley as the compliant element — series-elastic yaw. Pulley ratio + torsional stiffness/damping TODO (both bench-measurable). Sim model: SEA (rotor dof + torsional spring-damper to the yaw joint) or minimum-viable joint stiffness/damping; rigid-gear modeling would overstate steering bandwidth.

KNEE = ACTIVE WEAPON (user 2026-07-03): powered downward blade strike, gravity-assisted. Toggle-press force profile (huge near stowed, ~40 N + weight at -90 deg). The mesh_blade sim variant should model the knee as a strike actuator, not just a foot deployer — this mechanism, not the pneumatic rod, may be the real robot's primary attack.
